title: cuphea exilis
intro: Cuphea exilis, also known as the small cuphea or wild tobacco, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of cuphea exilis include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing coughs and sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
